multi_match_determinism: Attribution is DETERMINISTIC, not flaky: attributeDomain (ownership.go L250-265) returns the first matching domain over a slice that both listDomainDirs (docsync.go L366) and the divergence.go caller (L153 sort.Strings) sort lexicographically — no map iteration. So a file matched by two domains' paths: globs resolves to the alphabetically-first domain, stably across runs. The residual issue is silence, not nondeterminism: an overlapping glob picks a winner arbitrarily-by-name with no diagnostic. Current manifests (context-system/core/execution/workflow) are path-disjoint, so no live flap; and ADR-0036(f)/(i) already class resolved-file-set domain-overlap as an accepted gap with only a literal-string doctor Warn. NOT blocking, but the spec should add one sentence: 'multi-match resolves to the lexicographically-first domain dir; resolved-set overlap remains the ADR-0036(i) accepted gap.'
coverage_interaction: THIS IS THE BLOCKING GAP. R1 changes ONLY the bead-time per-file attribution (ValidateDivergence/attributeDomain) to glob across ALL domains. It does NOT touch the plan-time coverage gate: checkADRCoverage (plan.go L516) iterates loadImpactedDomains == meta.Domains (the prose ## Impacted Domains list, plan.go L730-735). The two gates therefore use DIFFERENT domain models — bead-gate = all-domains-by-glob, plan-gate = declared-impacted-by-name. Concrete new failure mode the spec never specs: a changed file globs (bead-time) to a REAL domain that is NOT in the spec's Impacted Domains; ValidateDivergence then calls coverageOf(nil, store, citations, thatDomain) (divergence.go L208); if no cited Accepted ADR declares that out-of-scope domain, the file now fires adr-divergence-uncovered (L234). Under today's code the same file attributes only if its domain is in meta.Domains, else it is adr-divergence-unowned. So R1 converts an unowned-failure into an uncovered-failure for out-of-declared-scope-but-owned files — same fail/pass polarity, but the spec's R1 text + the three ACs cover only (a) file-path/omitted entry now passes, (b) named-domain unchanged, (c) genuinely-unowned still unowned. The 'owned by a domain outside Impacted Domains, uncovered by citations' case is unspecified and untested, and the bead-gate(all-domains) vs plan-gate(impacted-only) asymmetry is never reconciled in the spec.
gate_weakening: The prompt's weakening worry is largely INVERTED by the code: R1 does not let an out-of-blast-radius edit silently pass — an out-of-scope file now attributes to its real owner and must have that owner covered by a cited Accepted ADR, so it still fails (as uncovered) unless the plan genuinely cites a covering Accepted ADR. The one real, narrow loosening: a bead edits files in domain X (e.g. execution) that is NOT in the spec's Impacted Domains, but the plan cites an Accepted ADR that happens to declare X as well as a declared domain — bead-gate now passes silently while the plan-gate never iterated X (not in Impacted Domains). That is arguably ZFC-correct (the citation truly covers X), but it does weaken the 'spec declares its blast radius' contract because the per-file gate no longer constrains edits to the DECLARED domain set, only to the OWNED+CITED set. The spec should make this an explicit, intended decision (it is currently silent on it) — either accept it in writing, or keep a lightweight out-of-declared-scope advisory.
blocking: Spec does not address the bead-gate(all-domains) vs plan-gate(impacted-only) domain-model split: R1 changes ValidateDivergence attribution but leaves checkADRCoverage keyed on meta.Domains. Spec the reconciliation explicitly — does the coverage/plan gate also move to all-domains, or is the asymmetry intended?

Missing the 'owned by an out-of-Impacted-Domains domain, NOT covered by any cited Accepted ADR' case: with R1 this file flips from adr-divergence-unowned to a NEW adr-divergence-uncovered. Add a fourth R1 acceptance criterion pinning this behavior (owned-but-out-of-scope-and-uncovered -> deterministic uncovered finding) so the change is a deliberate, tested decision rather than an emergent side effect.

Out-of-declared-blast-radius edit can now pass silently when the plan cites an Accepted ADR covering the (undeclared) owning domain. Record this loosening of the 'spec declares its blast radius' contract as an explicit intended decision in the R1 Design Decision section (or add a guard).
summary: R1's core direction is sound and ZFC-faithful: glob-match across all OWNERSHIP.yaml paths: is the literal ADR-0036(a) reading, and rejecting prefix-normalization is correct (prefix-guessing reintroduces the synthesized-fallback ZFC violation 091 removed). Multi-match is deterministic (first lexical domain dir), not flaky — manifests are disjoint today. Named-domain specs (099) resolve identically because globs are disjoint. The blocking gap is NOT in the chosen mechanism but in scope/consistency: R1 moves the bead-time divergence gate to all-domains-by-glob while leaving the plan-time coverage gate on the declared Impacted-Domains-by-name, creating a two-model split the spec never reconciles. That split introduces a new, unspecified, untested failure mode (out-of-Impacted-Domains owned file -> adr-divergence-uncovered instead of -unowned) and a narrow gate loosening (an undeclared-but-cited domain now passes silently). CHANGES: add the gate-consistency decision, a fourth R1 AC pinning the owned-out-of-scope-uncovered case, and an explicit ruling on the blast-radius loosening. No rework of the glob approach itself.
